tender.title: Supported Accommodation / Health Care for Children in Residential Care Feasibility Studies
tender.description:
As part of GM's successful Regional Care Cooperative bid there was a commitment to undertake feasibility studies focused on reimagining what the supported accommodation offer for 16 - 24 year olds (given the challenges that exist in terms of supply and suitability of accommodation) and the health care offer for children in residential care (given the variation in models and their impact). Both pieces of work will require extensive stakeholder engagement through interviews with local authorities, NHS colleagues, providers and young people alongside analysis of available data. This will require dedicated resources from people with the appropriate expertise and subject knowledge

****Issued as a direct award via exemption route as per the GMCA constitution ****

tender.title: North Northamptonshire Council - Provision of Dorking House Supported Accommodation
tender.description:
North Northamptonshire Council's  (hereafter referred to as the "Council") requirements for a service provider to wholly manage a Supported Housing Scheme in Corby called Dorking House which forms part of the Council's current rough sleeping pathway.

The Council are looking to allocate total grant funding of £902,562 to an organisation to provide a full housing management service for this supported accommodation facility. The intention is for Dorking House to be renamed/rebranded to reflect the new ethos of the new remodelled provision.

Dorking House will provide supported accommodation suitable for those with the highest and most complex of needs. The successful provider will ensure appropriate support services are delivered for adults with history of rough sleeping, and who have high levels of support needs.

Dorking House is made up of 3 Council owned Housing Revenue Account (HRA) properties, with a total of 9 bed spaces which have shared bathrooms and within one property there are 2 move-on flats which also has shared bathroom and kitchen facilities.

Overall, the aim of the provision will be to effectively support residents with complex needs by providing a safe and secure environment to help transform people's lives by encouraging self-determination and independence to achieve positive outcomes.

The Council is procuring a single provider to deliver the service for a maximum three-year period until 31st March 2027, with the option to extend for a further 12 months should further Government funding be secured.

The cohort being supported within this provision will include individuals with high complex support needs, and presenting risks, and therefore the provision will need to be adequately staffed to provide this level of support as required. The following is not an exhaustive list but provides examples of the cohort to be supported:

•	Entrenched rough sleepers
•	Individuals with a drug / alcohol dependence
•	Individuals experiencing mental ill health
•	Individuals with physical disabilities
•	Ex-offenders or individuals at risk of offending 
•	Individuals who are classed as too high a risk to be managed in other provisions within the rough sleeper pathway

The light touch regime (LTR) applies to the Services, as per Public Contracts Regulations 2015.

tender.title: Supported Accommodation and Floating Support Service for Young Children, Young People and Families or Parents to be
tender.description: The City of Doncaster Council (CDC) is seeking to commission a Supported Accommodation and Floating Support service for young children, young people and families or parents to be.  The successful provider should provide a safe, supportive environment and be committed to providing a sensitive, non-judgemental, and non-oppressive service. The provider will need to clearly demonstrate a commitment to multi-agency working and provide a holistic approach to ensure successful outcomes are achieved and sustained by the family.  The supported accommodation provision for the young person is intended to be of a short-term duration (maximum of 12 months) for young parent/s over 16 years of age up to 25 years of age. It is the intention that individuals will move on where appropriate in a planned manner. To this end the provider will develop the children, young people, and family's ability to live independently. The provision will deliver a programme of support. The level of support offered will vary and will be based on the ongoing assessed needs of each family. Needs will be assessed through the completion of either a early help assessment or social care assessment.   The supported accommodation aspect of the service will consist of a core building and dispersed property consisting of a minimum of 13 two-bedroom properties located within the City of Doncaster area (to be sourced by The Provider)  The floating support aspect of the Service will provide support for a minimum of 65 families at any one time (for a maximum of 12 months in respect of each family). This includes a whole family approach support package, for those who are either leaving the supported accommodation and require ongoing support to set up and maintain their tenancy as well as those who are already living in other arrangements

tender.title: Supported Accommodation and Support Service for People with Multiple Complex Needs
tender.description: The City of Doncaster Council is seeking to establish a contract to provide supported accommodation and a support service to vulnerable people with multiple complex needs. The Supported Accommodation and Support Service forms part of Doncaster's Homelessness Resettlement Pathway for individuals experiencing homelessness including those with complex needs. The service will offer high quality accommodation and be committed to providing a sensitive, strengths-based support service. The support provided to individuals is intended to be short-term (up to 12 months), with the aim of developing an individual's ability to live independently through the delivery of a tailored programme of support.

tender.title: Lot 27 East Hampshire Younger Adults Supported Accommodation
tender.description:
This contract will cover Younger Adults Supported Accommodation Services commissioned by Hampshire County Council. 

Younger Adults Supported Accommodation will have care and support attached to particular property.  This could be from a few hours a week to 24/7 support with a team of support staff. 

This Contract is initially for a capacity of 12 individuals.  The services are provided to 8 individuals in a block of 8 flats.  There are an additional 4 service users currently living in sole occupancy properties.  

There is likely to be future expansion of the Service if there is a requirement to support other individuals other properties in Hampshire. The Support Provider will be expected to work with the Council to help create Services that meet the needs of other individuals whose primary needs may fall within any of the client groups listed below. 

Client Groups 

The Client Groups that are eligible for these Services: 

People with a Learning Disability 

People with a Physical Disability/Sensory Impairment 

People with an enduring Mental Health condition 

People with Autism or Asperger's Syndrome 

At the point of tendering, the individuals identified have a learning disability.

The total value of the tender is worth up to £16,050,000 for up to 10 years, this includes the possibility of an expansion opportunity.  

The initial core service is worth up to £9,167,783 for up to 10 years. 

There may be scope for service expansion that will include another property(ies) and additional service users, worth up to £3,670,868 for up to 10 years. 

Awarded Value £8,427,648.60

tender.id: Category 2.5 Sheltered Housing Meals Provision Framework
tender.title: Category 2.5 Sheltered Housing Meals Provision Framework
tender.description:
This procurement has resulted in the award of a contract for frozen meals delivery to all Category 2.5 Sheltered Housing Schemes. The frozen meals allow the schemes to offer residents a wide range of meals to suit a variety of dietary requirements, including but not limited to vegetarian, reduced sugar and gluten free, ethical meals that include Halal, Kosher, Indian and Chinese. The meals are ordered by each scheme directly and they can then be reheated as and when they need to be served.

In total there are seven schemes that use the meal provision which serves over 350 service users. The average price per meal, including pudding, currently is approximately £3 per head.

The council has used the new ESPO framework (Ready Meals 58_23) for the award of a new contract. The service is awarding a contract for 3 years with the option to extended for a further 2 years.

tender.title: BHCC - JI - ITT - Affordable Housing Viability Study
tender.description:
We require specialist consultants to undertake an assessment of Affordable Housing viability. This will involve analysis of the potential financial viability of different types of housing development in Brighton & Hove and how this may affect the proportion of affordable housing that the Council could potentially seek through planning policy.  

The study will require testing the viability of a range of different development typologies on sites across the city to represent the types of sites that the Council expects to come forward. The exact scenarios to be tested will be agreed between the Council and the consultant once appointed. It will be necessary to consider the following key variables. 

Different scales of housing development (number of residential units) 

Types of housing development - e.g houses, flats, mixed sites including other uses (e.g commercial space, retail), specialised housing - e.g older persons housing, purpose-built housing for rent 

Site location (e.g central, suburban) and current use (e.g existing housing, vacant commercial, greenfield) 

For each development typology the study will need to test the viability of different amounts of affordable housing (zero to 50%) and different categories of affordable housing (e.g social rented, affordable rented, affordable housing for sale).   

 The development appraisals will need to assess housing development values and factor in assumptions about development costs including potential development contributions (including Community Infrastructure Levy (CIL) and development standards required by Council planning policies.

tender.title: Additional supported accommodation for Unaccompanied Asylum-Seeking Young People
tender.description:
Nottinghamshire County Council (the Council) commissioned a service to provide accommodation and support to Unaccompanied Asylum-Seeking Children (UASC) which commenced in August 2022.  However, the current demand for supported accommodation outweighs existing capacity and therefore the Council is looking to commission additional supported accommodation for USAC.\r
\r
The Council is seeking to establish a framework of suitable providers who are able to provide additional supported accommodation for UASC against two lots detailed in the service specification.\r
\r
The UASC service will offer safe accommodation and support to those requiring a planned transition into supported accommodation and to those requiring emergency accommodation further details of the services that will be purchased under the Framework by the Council are as set out in the service specification;\r
\r
An open multi-provider framework agreement will be put in place which means that during the framework Term the Council may re-open the Unaccompanied Asylum-Seeking Young People's Supported Accommodation Service tender exercise to run further competitive processes to allow new providers to be appointed to the framework.\r
\r
Bidders may tender for one or more of the following lots:\r
Lot 1: Core and Cluster - Single Provider Award\r
Lot 2: Emergency supported accommodation provision - Multi-Provider Award\r
\r
The framework agreement will be established with an initial duration of 5 years, with option to extend for a further three of up to two-year extensions to a maximum of 11 years with a proposed contract start date of the 1st of April 2023.\r
\r
This is a new competitive procurement process using an open procedure conducted in accordance with the light touch regime of the Public Procurement 2015 Regulations and advertised in the Find a Tender Service Find high value contracts in the public sector - GOV.UK (www.gov.uk)\r
\r
This opportunity can be accessed via the East Midlands Tenders portal https://www.eastmidstenders.org/  Reference DN643351\r
\r
Please read all the tender documents. If you have any questions, please submit them through the procurement portal using the messaging facility. The deadline for questions prior to tender submission is the 26th of January 2023, this is to allow time for questions to be answered before the deadline. \r
\r
The closing date for all tenders is at 15:30 hours on the 2nd of February 2023

tender.title: Domestic abuse accommodation and support for women with complex needs
tender.description:
The recommendations within Sefton's Domestic Abuse Needs Assessment regarding accommodation included:\r
â€¢	The safe accommodation offer in Sefton should be improved. Bed & Breakfast should not be used to house those affected by domestic abuse.\r
â€¢	Pathways and mechanisms into specialist domestic abuse services should be reviewed.\r
â€¢	Using Council of Europe calculation with minimum recommendation of one space per 10,000 population, this equates to 28 refuge spaces for Sefton.\r
Whilst Sefton already had a Complex Women's Service, this is within Homeless Services and does not specifically address domestic abuse. Sefton is looking to commission a Provider who can provide a person-centred, trauma-informed service specifically for women who have experience domestic abuse. The service will complement the Integrated Domestic Abuse Service and the Domestic Abuse Refuge.\r

tender.title: Supported Accommodation for Children Looked After and Care Leavers
tender.description:
\r
\r
Lewisham Council (the Council) invites you to submit a Tender to provide Supported Accommodation for Children Looked After and Care Leavers aged 16-25 years-old in Lewisham. This contract will be procured through a two-stage restricted process in accordance with the Light Touch Regime of the Public Contract Regulations (2015).  There will be two stages to the tender process.  The first stage will require all interested organisations to complete the Standard Selection Questionnaire (SSQ).  This includes selection questions on the tenderer's relevant experience. The Standard Selection Questionnaires received will then be evaluated by the Council. This will determine which organisations will be shortlisted and invited through to the second stage.
